context.description: A philosophical book page asks why people focus on other dream philosophers instead of the dream itself. It contrasts medical improvement with a deeper notion of clarity in awareness and questions how such strategies should be measured.
normalization_notes: Mapped raw category/subcategory to the closest active profile codes for philosophical book prose.
Translated visible German prose into English control-language summary and tags.

fields.section_heading: Why do so many interested people become dream philosophers?

rows.paragraph_text: A reflective passage argues that people notice what worsens but overlook what improves, using the idea of a "crystal brain" to question whether unnoticed functional improvement would even be recorded. It distinguishes therapeutic well-being from a sharper, clearer awareness of one's own information streams and asks how such clarity should be measured. The section then criticizes dream philosophers for talking about each other instead of the dream itself, ending in an unfinished meditation on human nature and altered states.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Book page from Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2 on consciousness, clarity, perception, and criticism of dream philosophers. Section heading: Why do so many interested people become dream philosophers?
